channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Switzerland advances to World Cup quarters for first time since 1954 after penalty shootout victory over Colombia
item.description: Switzerland stunned Colombia in a penalty shootout during the FIFA 2026 World Cup round of 16, securing their first World Cup quarter-final berth since 1954. The match, held in Vancouver, ended 0-0 af...

item.title: Norwegian Rider Traeen Claims Yellow Jersey as Pedersen Wins Fourth Stage of Tour de France
item.description: In a thrilling development during the 113th Tour de France, Norwegian rider Torstein Træen secured the coveted yellow jersey after a dramatic stage win, while Denmark's Mads Pedersen claimed victory i...

item.title: Four Officials Removed from Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav's Office
item.description: The article reports that four senior officials were recently removed from the office of Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav. The dismissals include a principal secretary, a director general, and two ...

item.title: LAPD withholds officer's identity in Canoga Park dog shooting amid legal dispute
item.description: The Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) has refused to release the identity of an officer involved in the fatal shooting of a golden Saint Berdoodle in Canoga Park, citing threats to the officer's sa...

item.title: US launches strikes on Iran's military targets amid oil sanctions and shipping attacks
item.description: The US military conducted new strikes on Iran's military infrastructure, targeting air defense systems, coastal surveillance, and missile sites, following the reinstatement of oil sanctions after thre...

item.title: Black female academics rely on White male mentors for career advancement – study reveals
item.description: A study reveals that Black female academics in South Africa often rely on White male professors for career advancement, despite systemic barriers. The research, conducted by a PhD candidate in social ...

item.title: Helen Rose: Pioneering Costume Designer of Hollywood's Golden Age
item.description: Helen Rose (1904–1985) was a legendary American costume designer who became one of the most influential figures in Hollywood during its Golden Age. As head costume designer at Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer (MGM...

item.title: FG plans demolitions on Lagos Coastal Highway to combat flooding
item.description: The Federal Government is planning to demolish some buildings along Lagos Coastal Highway to improve drainage and reduce flooding. Works Minister David Umahi stated that the highway isn't the main cau...

item.title: Philippines’ Upper-Middle-Income Status Brings Greater Governance and Institutional Challenges
item.description: The article argues that the World Bank’s decision to reclassify the Philippines as an upper-middle-income economy should be seen as the beginning of a more demanding stage of national development rath...

item.title: Investment banks face criticism for overwhelmingly positive support of SpaceX's planned IPO
item.description: The article argues that Wall Street’s investment banks have demonstrated an unusually strong consensus in support of SpaceX ahead of what is expected to become the world’s largest initial public offer...

item.title: Edmonton Adjusts Bike Lane Plans After Resident Protests
item.description: Residents of Edmonton's 50th Street area staged a protest against the city's plan to remove on-street parking to install bike lanes, leading to revised proposals. The original plan aimed to eliminate ...

item.title: March and March Movement Launches Three-Month Campaign Against Irregular Migration
item.description: The March and March movement has unveiled a three-month action plan to intensify its campaign against irregular migration, following the nationwide protests on June 30. The movement, which credited ci...

item.title: Djokovic Triumphs in Epic Wimbledon Quarter-Final Against Auger-Aliassime
item.description: Novak Djokovic secured a dramatic five-hour victory over Pablo Carreño Busta at Wimbledon, showcasing his enduring prowess on the grass courts. The match, which stretched to four sets and a deciding t...

item.title: Feminism's Backlash Evolves: From #NotAllMen to Accusations of 'Man-Hating'
item.description: The article explores the shifting dynamics of backlash against feminism, noting a transition from the #NotAllMen narrative to accusations of 'man-hating'. Jane Gilmore, an ex-journalist and feminist w...

item.title: Argentina overcome 2-0 deficit to defeat Egypt and reach World Cup quarters
item.description: In a thrilling FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 clash at Atlanta Stadium, Argentina staged a remarkable comeback from a 2-0 deficit to defeat Egypt 3-2 and secure their place in the quarter-finals. The...

item.title: Imee Marcos' Reaction During Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial
item.description: The article discusses Senator Imee Marcos' reaction during the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte. During the trial, Duterte's testimony included allegations against her late father, for...

item.title: ED probes Kolkata firm linked to aircraft deal and TMC funds
item.description: The Economic Offenses Department (ED) has initiated an investigation into a Kolkata-based company suspected of receiving Rs 160 crore from the Tata Motors Corporation (TMC) in connection with an aircr...

item.title: Alberta Town Tofield Begins Flood Recovery Efforts After Heavy Rainfall
item.description: The town of Tofield, Alberta, is in the early stages of cleanup following severe flooding caused by heavy rainfall in late June 2026. Mayor Adam Hall confirmed that floodwaters have largely receded, w...

item.title: By-election in Clacton to proceed despite major parties' boycott, experts predict Farage's likely victory
item.description: The Clacton by-election, triggered by Nigel Farage's resignation, is set to proceed despite a boycott by all major political parties. Polling expert Prof Sir John Curtice has stated that Farage cannot...

item.title: State of Origin analysis: Key stats highlight Laurie Daley's challenges
item.description: The NSW Blues face a crucial State of Origin decider against Queensland at Suncorp Stadium, with Laurie Daley's performance under intense scrutiny. Analysts have compiled six key charts to assess the ...
